input_row.title: Ensure 'Enforce SSL connection' is set to 'Enabled' for Standard MySQL Database Server
input_row.description: Enable SSL connection on MYSQL Servers.
input_row.rationale: SSL connectivity helps to provide a new layer of security by connecting database server to client applications using Secure Sockets Layer (SSL). Enforcing SSL connections between database server and client applications helps protect against "man in the middle" attacks by encrypting the data stream between the server and application.
input_row.audit: From Azure Portal 1. Login to Azure Portal using https://portal.azure.com 2. Go to Azure Database for MySQL servers 3. For each database, click on Connection security 4. In SSL settings, ensure Enforce SSL connection is set to ENABLED. From Azure CLI Ensure the output of the below command returns ENABLED. az mysql server show --resource-group <resourceGroupName> --name <serverName> --query sslEnforcement  From Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: e802a67a-daf5-4436-9ea6-f6d821dd0c5d - Name: 'Enforce SSL connection should be enabled for MySQL database servers'
input_row.remediation: From Azure Portal 1. Login to Azure Portal using https://portal.azure.com 2. Go to Azure Database for MySQL servers 3. For each database, click on Connection security 4. In SSL settings, click on ENABLED to Enforce SSL connections From Azure CLI Use the below command to set MYSQL Databases to Enforce SSL connection. az mysql server update --resource-group <resourceGroupName> --name <serverName> --ssl-enforcement Enabled Default Value: Azure Database for MySQL when provisioned through the Azure portal or CLI will require SSL connections by default. References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/mysql/single-server/concepts-ssl- connection-security 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/mysql/single-server/how-to-configure-ssl 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-data- protection#dp-3-encrypt-sensitive-data-in-transit

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions specific Azure CLI commands (`az mysql server show` and `az mysql server update`), which indicates that the property can be checked programmatically.
- The SSL enforcement setting is a standard Azure Database for MySQL server property.
- This control does not require SSH or file system access, nor does it involve human judgment to verify compliance.
- The rationale connects the setting directly to security best practices and mentions API/CLI commands for verification.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_mysql_server_ssl_enforcement_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **Use Azure CLI:** 
   - Commands to check the SSL enforcement settings on MySQL servers.
   - The command `az mysql server show --resource-group <resourceGroupName> --name <serverName> --query sslEnforcement` will be used to validate if SSL enforcement is set to "ENABLED".

2. **Enumerate resources:**
   - The program should enumerate all MySQL servers within specified resource groups or subscriptions.

3. **Configuration Properties:**
   - Validate the property `sslEnforcement` to check if it equals "ENABLED" for each MySQL server.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** If `sslEnforcement` is "ENABLED".
   - **Fail:** If `sslEnforcement` is not "ENABLED" or the server does not exist.

5. **Example CLI Command:**
   - `az mysql server show --resource-group <resourceGroupName> --name <serverName> --query sslEnforcement --output tsv`
   - This command will return the value of `sslEnforcement`, which can be evaluated for compliance.
